insight: For Jordan, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.150 minutes per break, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when Jordan is pressing before a break, they tend to lose more momentum afterwards, with an average change of -0.300 minutes per break, and they were unable to regain their momentum after the break in the one instance where this happened. In contrast, when Jordan is not pressing before a break, they are able to maintain their momentum, with no change in their performance after the break, which could be a valuable strategic insight for their coaching team.
